pattern: Grouping indices by value and only comparing each index to its circular neighbors within that group finds every nearest-equal distance in linear time.
hints: Only indices that share the same value can ever answer each other's query, so group indices by value first.
Within one value's sorted list of positions, the nearest match for any position is always its neighbor in that list, wrapping around the array's ends.
For each position, compute the circular distance to its previous and next same-value position in the sorted group and keep the smaller; a value with only one occurrence answers -1.

edge_inputs.description: single element array, no other index can match
all values equal so neighbors wrap around trivially
matching value sits at opposite ends, distance is via the wraparound not the long way
a value with exactly three occurrences to test neighbor selection
every value distinct so every query answers -1
repeated query indices should just repeat the answer

stress.description: large circular array with a narrow value range to force many duplicate groups
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 2000))
    nums = [rng.randint(1, max(2, n // 10)) for _ in range(n)]
    qcount = max(1, min(n, 500))
    queries = [rng.randrange(0, n) for _ in range(qcount)]
    return [nums, queries]
